description: Lock Muskets (Flintlock) to true ca. 1620 French flintlock firearm lockwork, not generic early firearms, mechanical clocks, or later percussion/rifled-musket systems.

checks.reason: The node is anchored to the Met's ca. 1620 French Louis XIII flintlock gun.
Earlier hand firearms and harquebuses remain the historical firearm lineage.
Early firearms supply lineage rather than a direct engineering subsystem for true flintlock lockwork.
The flintlock depends on spring-and-sear lockwork rather than clocks as timekeeping devices.
Clockwork-style lock mechanisms supply an enabling mechanical craft capability.
Mechanical clocks should not be restored as a direct prerequisite unless the node is narrowed to clockmaking transfer evidence.
Steel/iron striking surfaces, springs, barrels, and lock parts remain enabling material capabilities.
Metallurgy enables reliable components but is not the sole invention path.
